fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 31-year-old White male with schizophrenia for medication review.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing casual and cooperative. States he takes his quetiapine 300 mg at bedtime consistently without missing doses, tracked in phone app. Reports feeling stable overall. Denies current hallucinations. No paranoid ideation. Sleep good at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite normal, weight steady at 178 lbs. Working part-time at grocery store stocking shelves overnight, job going well for past eight months. Mood 'doing okay' rated 6/10. Lives in apartment with supportive roommate. Enjoys video gaming and watching sports. Attends young adult mental health group twice monthly. Three years sober from marijuana. Father visits weekly for dinner which patient enjoys. Mild social anxiety in crowds but manageable. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Evaluated by Karen Sullivan, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good hygiene, calm demeanor

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with roommate; employed part-time; three years sober; attending support group; family involvement.
Assessment_and_Plan: Good medication adherence using app tracking.
fields.Author: Note written by Karen Sullivan, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Northside Behavioral Care, 3421 Main Street, Milwaukee, WI 53202, Phone: (555) 884-2217, Fax: (555) 884-2218
